name: Meowdulator
description: The Meowdulator is a very silly pedal that makes cat noises. It only carries a single octave worth of meows, so it is very limited for use with chords. All the knobs are labeled 'Meow'. There are a few different cat voices to choose from. This pedal is a special co-promotion with B's Music, so it has the B's Music logo (on the microphone).

name: Atreides
description: The name comes from the 'weirding module' invented for David Lynch's Dune, and it is that strange. Kind of a fuzz, but also kind of a synthesizer, it's more of a toy than a tone shaper.

name: Bass Synth
description: If you need that synthesizer bass sound from your bass, this will do it. Better at handling low end frequencies than other synth pedals.

name: NS-2 Noise Suppressor
description: Does exactly what the name says. Most effective when noisy pedals are contained in a four-cable send and return loop. But can also go anywhere in the chain and filter other kinds of noise.

name: Cry Baby
description: The classic, base-model wah. No frills, all analog.

name: Ditto X2
description: The Ditto is a full-featured looping pedal. You can slow down or reverse the loop, but my favorite feature is the easy stop.

name: Fuzz Factory
description: Five controls for a fuzz pedal is a lot, and this one is capable of dialing in a ton of custom sounds. When I got it used, some of the original black knobs were damaged, so I replaced all of them with multi-colored knobs. It seemed to fit the pedal.

name: Garbage Face
description: This fuzz is a collaboration between the manufacturer and J Mascis, the guitarist from Dinosaur Jr. There are two separate volume controls for the fuzz, and you can switch between them. In true J Mascis style, there's also a treble boost that you can tap for a little extra power.

name: Ghost Writer
description: The Ghost Writer is a simple transformer that allows you to use a guitar as a MIDI controller. This allows you to plug it into a synthesizer or other MIDI device. Comes with a special cable just for this purpose.

name: Iridium
description: Iridium is an amp-in-a-box pedal that lets you dial in a custom amplifier and cabinet profile. It has a headphone jack, so you can use it to play without an actual amplifier.

name: Melon Cube
description: This fuzz has a lot of options for shaping its sound. More friendly sounding than some of the darker muff-style fuzzes out there.

name: Pulp 'N' Peel
description: This compressor will squeeze fresh orange juice out of your guitar. There's also a hidden rat-type distortion circuit that can be activated using bonus controls. This pedal has a huge number of features for its size.

name: Spring King
description: Back in the 60s, surf guitar players would kick their reverb tanks to make the springs inside make a really cool crashing sound. Most modern reverb pedals don't have real springs in them, so it's harder to make that sound. Enter the Spring King: a simple spring reverb that has real springs, and a special kick plate that you can tap with your foot to recreate the sound.

name: Acapulco Gold
description: This powerful distortion, with just one massive level knob, can be dangerous to animals and small children. One of the loudest pedals I've ever used.

name: BD-2 Blues Driver
description: Not sure why they call it a 'Blues' driver, but this overdrive is very versatile and has become quite popular with alternative rock and shoegaze guitarists.

name: Volume Pedal
description: This analog pedal is pretty simple. The foot switch rocks back and forth, with full volume at one end, and mute at the other. Essential for fades and swells.

name: MF-4
description: Fuzzlord makes just one kind of pedal: fuzz. This one has a really grimey and doomy sound, and it cleans up nicely with a distortion nearby. Artwork is... controversial.

name: Green Screamer
description: Every pedal company has their own version of the Ibanez Tube Screamer circuit, and they often make the pedal green to let you know exactly what to expect. I like this one better than most.

name: HM-2w Heavy Metal
description: The original HM-2 was very popular in Nordic countries for some reason, and became the foundation of the Swedish death metal and Norwegian black metal scenes. There's just one way to use this pedal - turn everything up to maximum.

name: Hizumitas
description: A lot of heavy fuzz for such a small pedal with a cute graphic. Apparently based on a custom circuit from a Japanese doom metal artist. Pairs nicely with Plumes.

name: Katzenkönig
description: The name is German for 'cat king', an appropriate name for a ToneBender-style fuzz crossed with a Rat distortion. I like this one because it is lighter than the usual muff-style fuzzes.

name: Kompressor
description: There are a lot of compressors out there, but this one has a lot of knobs. And it's black and looks like metal, which is probably why it has become popular with metal guitarists.

name: Lillian
description: Walrus makes a whole line of tone-shaping effects named after women. There's probably a whole story of who Lillian is. Regardless, this is a very robust phaser.

name: MT-2w Metal Zone
description: The Metal Zone gets a lot of hate for having a thin and shrill sound, but there's a lot more going on here. Name another pedal with four tone controls! If you need to make a Telecaster and a keyboard amp sound like metal, this will do it. And even if you have all your distortion needs managed, this pedal can still be used as an instant feedback circuit.

name: Muffuletta
description: JHS likes to give you a lot of bang for your buck, and this pedal is no exception. If you can't figure out which style of Big Muff you want to buy, they've got you covered. The lower right knob allows you to switch between six different Big Muff sounds, including Pi, Russian, Ram's Head, and more. Named after a delicious deli sandwich that is popular in New Orleans.

name: OS-2 Overdrive Distortion
description: This is a great pedal for beginners. It's capable of a lot of different rock sounds, right out of the box, regardless of your amp or guitar. Pairs well with other distortions. One of the oldest pedals in my collection.

name: Plumes
description: Plumes is my absolute favorite overdrive pedal. It's similar to a Tube Screamer topology, but it's capable of so much more. Pairs nicely with Hizumitas. This is a limited edition color called 'water blue'.

name: Sabbra Cadabra
description: Tony Iommi from Black Sabbath had an instantly recognizable sound, created by his custom Laney amps. If you want that sound, but can't find or afford a vintage amp, this pedal reproduces it like magic.

name: Spark
description: This mini pedal does just one thing, and does it well: boosts your volume. Use it after a volume sucking pedal, or as a boost going into another pedal. Either way, you're guaranteed to be louder.

name: Star Crash
description: This is a Fuzz Face circuit, but with a little bit more volume and some nifty shaping options. Sounds just like the 70s.

name: SY-1 Synthesizer
description: If you want your guitar to go beep-boop, start here. The Boss has eight different sound types, with ten variations of each, which lets you create dozens of sounds. And it has send/return and expression jacks for even more versatility.

name: Sentry
description: The Sentry locks down your sound with the best noise gate on the market. Most effective when noisy pedals are contained in a four-cable send and return loop. Absolutely essential if you have noisy fuzzes and loud distortions in your chain.

name: TU-3w
description: Boss has been the leader in pedal tuners for decades. And this one's black! I like it because it's responsive enough to reliably tune basses and downtuned guitars.
